text: The water cycle, also known as the hydrological cycle, describes the continuous movement of water on, above, and below Earth's surface. The main stages are evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and collection. Evaporation occurs when the sun heats surface water in oceans, rivers, and lakes, turning it into water vapor that rises into the atmosphere. Condensation happens when water vapor cools and forms clouds. Precipitation is when water falls back to Earth as rain, snow, sleet, or hail. Collection refers to water gathering in oceans, rivers, lakes, and groundwater. The sun drives the water cycle by providing the energy needed for evaporation. The water cycle plays a critical role in distributing heat around the planet and supporting all life on Earth.
